title: In re Target Corp. Customer Data Security Breach Litigation (2014) — MDL and Eighth Circuit
forum: U.S. District Court (D. Minn.) / Eighth Circuit
technical_focus: POS and store network security (high level)

timeline.event: Public disclosure of major payment card / customer data breach
District court issues 66 F. Supp. 3d 1154 (motion to dismiss rulings)
Eighth Circuit issues 847 F.3d 608 (class certification rigor / remand)

primary_sources.label: D. Minn. opinion (66 F. Supp. 3d 1154) — CourtListener

evidence_artifacts: Network segmentation diagrams and firewall baselines (store/POS segments)
Vendor remote access logs and approvals
Centralized logging and retention policies aligned to legal hold
Incident response tickets and executive communications (privileged review)
engineering_takeaways: Retail payment environments can drive decade-scale MDL litigation; logging and change control matter for disputes.
Class certification outcomes can turn on rigorous, specific findings and appellate review standards.
